authors: D.O. Potyondy; P.A. Cundall
title: A bonded-particle model for rock
journal: International Journal of Rock Mechanics and Mining Sciences
use: Methods/L6 foundation: defines the parallel-bond BPM and the bond-breakage-equals-microcrack convention that anchors our micro-damage definition for lining/rock DEM.

authors: N. Cho; C.D. Martin; D.C. Sego
title: A clumped particle model for rock
journal: International Journal of Rock Mechanics and Mining Sciences
use: Calibration-caveats paragraph: documents the classic BPM defects (too-low UCS/tensile ratio and friction angle) that justify our clump/flat-joint contact-model choice.

authors: Jeoungseok Yoon
title: Application of experimental design and optimization to PFC model calibration in uniaxial compression simulation
journal: International Journal of Rock Mechanics and Mining Sciences
use: Calibration-workflow paragraph: the canonical experimental-design/optimization template for fitting PFC micro-parameters to uniaxial-compression macro-response.

authors: David Oskar Potyondy
title: The bonded-particle model as a tool for rock mechanics research and application: current trends and future directions
use: Intro/state-of-the-art paragraph: authoritative review of BPM variants (parallel-bond, flat-joint, smooth-joint) framing why our DEM contact model is selected.

authors: Shunchuan Wu; Xueliang Xu
title: A Study of Three Intrinsic Problems of the Classic Discrete Element Method Using Flat-Joint Model
use: Methods-justification paragraph: shows the flat-joint model resolves the three intrinsic BPM problems (UCS/TS ratio, friction angle, linear strength envelope), backing our calibration targets.

authors: M. Nitka; J. Tejchman
title: Modelling of concrete behaviour in uniaxial compression and tension with DEM
use: Concrete-lining DEM paragraph: meso-scale DEM calibration of concrete in both uniaxial compression and tension, anchoring the lining-material micro-parameter set.

authors: Jun Peng; Louis Ngai Yuen Wong; Cee Ing Teh
title: Effects of grain size-to-particle size ratio on micro-cracking behavior using a bonded-particle grain-based model
journal: International Journal of Rock Mechanics and Mining Sciences
use: Micro-damage-definition paragraph: quantifies how grain/particle size ratio controls simulated micro-cracking, guiding our particle-resolution and crack-counting choices.

authors: Matthew Tsang; Ian Clark; Jurij Karlovšek
title: Automating the Calibration of Flat-Jointed Bonded Particle Model Microproperties for the Rewan Sandstone Case Study
use: Calibration-automation sentence: recent (2023) automated flat-joint micro-property calibration workflow, evidencing reproducible fitting practice in the same journal family.

authors: Chiu Ya-Chu; Lee Chia-Han; Wang Tai-Tien
title: Lining crack evolution of an operational tunnel influenced by slope instability
use: Introduction/case-motivation paragraph: canonical slope-tunnel system evidence that progressive slope instability drives lining crack evolution in an operating tunnel (Wang Tai-Tien group, Taiwan case lineage).

authors: Tang Kunjie; Qiu Junling; Lai Jinxing; Xue Fangchen; Wang Zhichao; Li Xing
title: Experimental investigation on deformation-failure mechanisms of a shallow-bias large-section loess tunnel induced by rainfall
use: Rainfall-infiltration loading paragraph: model-test proof that rainfall infiltration produces asymmetric bias loading and failure of a slope-side tunnel, the macro-scale forcing in the cross-scale chain.

authors: Sun Weixin; Han Fucheng; Zhang Yanmei; Zhang Wengang; Zhang Runhong; Su Weijia
title: Experimental assessment of structural responses of tunnels under the groundwater level fluctuation
use: Groundwater-fluctuation boundary-condition paragraph: experimental lining structural response to water-table rise/fall, justifying transient external-water-load cycles in the model.

authors: Tan Yuqi; Smith John V.; Li Chun-Qing; Currell Matthew; Wu Yufei
title: Predicting external water pressure and cracking of a tunnel lining by measuring water inflow rate
use: External-water-pressure quantification paragraph: field-measurable inflow-rate to external-pressure to lining-cracking chain used to calibrate the seepage load on the lining.

authors: Yu Jun; Li Dongkai; Zheng Jingfan; Zhang Zhizhong; He Zhen; Fan Yong
title: Analytical study on the seepage field of different drainage and pressure relief options for tunnels in high water-rich areas
use: Drainage/pressure-relief design paragraph: analytical seepage-field solutions for drainage schemes, the benchmark for external-water-pressure reduction in the discussion of countermeasures.

authors: Causse Lionel; Cojean Roger; Fleurisson Jean-Alain
title: Interaction between tunnel and unstable slope - Influence of time-dependent behavior of a tunnel excavation in a deep-seated gravitational slope deformation
use: Slope-tunnel interaction mechanism paragraph: time-dependent DSGSD-tunnel interaction supporting the long-term creep-plus-groundwater coupling framing.

authors: Jia Jingqi; Chen Yun; Luo Hao; Ma Guowei
title: Seepage stability analysis of a deep-buried tunnel in fractured rocks based on a non-Darcy hydro-mechanical coupled method
use: Hydro-mechanical methodology paragraph: non-Darcy HM-coupled seepage stability in fractured rock, backing the choice of coupled fracture-flow formulation at the meso scale.

authors: Zhang Sulei; Xu Qing; Yoo Chungsik; Min Bo; Liu Chang; Guan Xiaoming; Li Pengfei
title: Lining cracking mechanism of old highway tunnels caused by drainage system deterioration: A case study of Liwaiao Tunnel, Ningbo, China
use: Lining-deterioration/serviceability paragraph: drainage clogging leading to water-pressure build-up and cracking in ageing tunnels, feeding the degradation and maintenance implications section.

authors: Bai, Yifan; Li, Xiaojing; Yang, Weimin; Xu, Zhenhao; Lv, Mingying
title: Multiscale analysis of tunnel surrounding rock disturbance: A PFC3D-FLAC3D coupling algorithm with the overlapping domain method
use: Methods coupling-scheme paragraph: the direct PFC3D-FLAC3D overlapping-domain precedent that legitimizes embedding a bonded-particle near-field inside a continuum far-field for tunnel disturbance analysis.

authors: Zhou, Zongqing; Gao, Tian; Sun, Jiwei; Gao, Chenglu; Bai, Songsong; Jin, Gaohan; Liu, Yuhan
title: An FDM-DEM coupling method based on REV for stability analysis of tunnel surrounding rock
use: Introduction state-of-the-art paragraph: the most-cited recent TUST FDM-DEM coupling paper (REV-based domain partition) — anchors the 'why couple continuum and discrete' argument in the target journal itself.

authors: Liu, Quansheng; Sun, Lei
title: Simulation of coupled hydro-mechanical interactions during grouting process in fractured media based on the combined finite-discrete element method
use: Introduction lineage paragraph: high-citation TUST FDEM paper proving the journal's tradition of combined finite-discrete coupled simulation for underground works.

authors: Vazaios, I.; Vlachopoulos, N.; Diederichs, M.S.
title: Assessing fracturing mechanisms and evolution of excavation damaged zone of tunnels in interlocked rock masses at high stresses using a finite-discrete element approach
journal: Journal of Rock Mechanics and Geotechnical Engineering
use: Introduction/discussion benchmark paragraph: highest-cited FDEM tunnel-EDZ study; the reviewer-expected reference showing discrete fracture prediction outperforms pure continuum for excavation damage.

authors: Lisjak, Andrea; Tatone, Bryan S. A.; Mahabadi, Omid K.; Grasselli, Giovanni; Marschall, Paul; Lanyon, George W.; Vaissière, Rémi de la; Shao, Hua; Leung, Helen; Nussbaum, Christophe
title: Hybrid Finite-Discrete Element Simulation of the EDZ Formation and Mechanical Sealing Process Around a Microtunnel in Opalinus Clay
use: Introduction founding-lineage paragraph: the classic Lisjak-Grasselli hybrid FDEM tunnel study establishing discrete simulation of damage evolving around an excavation and interacting with the support/sealing system.

authors: Liu, Chang; Zhang, Dingli; Fang, Qian; Zhang, Sulei; Sun, Zhenyu
title: Investigation of progressive failure mechanism of tunnel lining with material defects using discrete element method
use: Core precedent for the bonded-particle lining section: DEM model of a concrete lining with material defects reproducing progressive crack initiation-coalescence — the closest published analogue to our particle-based lining.

authors: Rasmussen, Leandro Lima; Min, Ki-Bok
title: Hybrid lattice/discrete element analysis of spalling failure in rock tunnels
use: Methods mixed-fidelity paragraph: recent TUST hybrid lattice/DEM tunnel-spalling study supporting the strategy of reserving expensive discrete resolution for the failure-prone zone only.

authors: Wang, Tuo; Huang, Hongwei; Zhang, Fengshou; Han, Yanhui
title: DEM-continuum mechanics coupled modeling of slot-shaped breakout in high-porosity sandstone
use: Methods boundary-handshake paragraph: TUST precedent (Huang/Zhang group) for a wall-coupled DEM inset embedded in a continuum far-field, validating our two-way force-displacement exchange scheme.

authors: Bai, Songsong; Zhou, Zongqing; Gao, Chenglu; Gao, Tian; Jin, Gaohan; Tao, Guangzhe
title: An FDM–DEM bridging domain coupling simulation method considering blasting dynamic disturbance for tunnel excavation
use: Methods/discussion coupling-refinement paragraph: newest bridging-domain FDM-DEM variant handling dynamic disturbance across the coupling boundary — cites forward to show the interface-treatment problem our scheme addresses.

authors: Fahimifar, A.; Zareifard, M.R.
title: A theoretical solution for analysis of tunnels below groundwater considering the hydraulic-mechanical coupling
use: Analytical hydraulic-mechanical coupled solution for tunnels below groundwater table; baseline for pore-pressure/seepage effects on lining loads

authors: Tarifard, A.; Gorog, P.; Torok, A.
title: Long-term assessment of creep and water effects on tunnel lining loads in weak rocks using displacement-based direct back analysis: an example from northwest of Iran
journal: Geomechanics and Geophysics for Geo-Energy and Geo-Resources
use: Combined creep + water effect on long-term lining loads via displacement-based back analysis; direct precedent for coupled creep-water lining-load assessment

authors: Li, A.; Deng, H.; Zhang, H.; Jiang, M.; Liu, H.; Xiao, Y.; Wen, J.
title: Developing a two-step improved damage creep constitutive model based on soft rock saturation-loss cycle triaxial creep test
use: Laboratory saturation-loss cyclic triaxial creep evidence and damage creep constitutive model for soft rock; supports water-cycle-driven creep degradation

authors: Yan, B.; Guo, Q.; Ren, F.; Cai, M.
title: Modified Nishihara model and experimental verification of deep rock mass under the water-rock interaction
journal: International Journal of Rock Mechanics and Mining Sciences
use: Modified Nishihara viscoelastic-viscoplastic model incorporating water-rock interaction for deep rock; constitutive basis for water-weakened creep

authors: Ma, G.; He, Z.; He, C.; Kang, X.; Wang, S.; Xu, G.
title: Time-dependent performance assessment of mountain tunnels considering the hazards associated with squeezing so (full title: Time-dependent performance assessment of mountain tunnels considering the hazards associated with squeezing and nonuniform steel corrosion of RC lining)
use: Time-dependent performance of mountain-tunnel RC lining under squeezing plus nonuniform steel corrosion; long-term serviceability/durability coupling precedent

authors: Wang, Y.; Liu, Y.; Wang, Z.-F.; Zhang, X.; Hui, Y.; Li, J.
title: Investigation on progressive failure process of tunnel lining induced by creep effect of surrounding rock: A case study
use: Case study of progressive lining failure driven by surrounding-rock creep; field evidence for creep-induced lining cracking sequence

authors: Sharifzadeh, M.; Tarifard, A.; Moridi, M.A.
title: Time-dependent behavior of tunnel lining in weak rock mass based on displacement back analysis method
use: Displacement back-analysis of time-dependent lining behavior in weak rock; classic TUST reference for creep-parameter identification from monitoring

authors: Kovacevic, M.S.; Bacic, M.; Gavin, K.; Stipanovic, I.
title: Assessment of long-term deformation of a tunnel in soft rock by utilizing particle swarm optimized neural network
use: PSO-optimized neural network prediction of long-term tunnel deformation; data-driven long-term deformation forecasting precedent in TUST

authors: Yin, Z.; Zhang, X.; Li, X.; Zhang, J.; Zhang, Q.
title: Modified Burgers model of creep behavior of grouting-reinforced body and its long-term effect on tunnel operation
use: Modified Burgers creep model for grouting-reinforced body and its long-term effect during tunnel operation; rheological model for reinforced ground

authors: Wu, K.; Song, J.; Zhao, N.; Shao, Z.
title: Study on the time-dependent interaction between surrounding rock and yielding supports in deep soft rock tunnels
journal: International Journal for Numerical and Analytical Methods in Geomechanics
use: Time-dependent rock-yielding-support interaction analysis for deep soft rock tunnels (Saint-Martin-la-Porte type squeezing conditions); yielding-support design under creep

authors: Barla, G.; Debernardi, D.; Sterpi, D.
title: Time-Dependent Modeling of Tunnels in Squeezing Conditions
use: SHELVIP-type time-dependent modeling of severely squeezing tunnels (Saint Martin La Porte); benchmark viscoplastic squeezing analysis

authors: Zheng, C.; Zhu, X.; Zhang, Z.
title: Damage evolution of tunnel lining under creep action considering interlayer effect in gently inclined layered surrounding rock
use: Lining damage evolution under creep with interlayer effect in gently inclined layered rock; anisotropic/layered creep loading of lining

authors: Paraskevopoulou, C.; Diederichs, M.
title: Analysis of time-dependent deformation in tunnels using the Convergence-Confinement Method
use: Convergence-confinement framework extended to time-dependent deformation; methodological anchor for long-term ground-support interaction curves

authors: Cai, M.; Kaiser, P.K.; Tasaka, Y.; Maejima, T.; Morioka, H.; Minami, M.
title: Generalized crack initiation and crack damage stress thresholds of brittle rock masses near underground excavations
journal: International Journal of Rock Mechanics and Mining Sciences
use: Generalized crack-initiation (CI) and crack-damage (CD) stress thresholds; basis for damage-threshold criteria in long-term strength degradation

authors: Sulem, J.; Panet, M.; Guenot, A.
title: An analytical solution for time-dependent displacements in a circular tunnel
journal: International Journal of Rock Mechanics and Mining Sciences & Geomechanics Abstracts
use: Classic closed-form solution combining face-advance and time effects on circular-tunnel convergence; foundation of semi-empirical convergence law

authors: Liu, B.; Song, Y.; Chu, Z.
title: Time-dependent safety of lining structures of circular tunnels in weak rock strata
journal: International Journal of Mining Science and Technology
use: Time-dependent safety evaluation of circular-tunnel linings in weak strata; viscoelastic analytical lining-load evolution and safety-factor decay
